paragraphs.text: Student of the Jean Monnet Modules project “DERECHO”: ‘Defense of European Rights and Excellence for Consumers HHarmonisation’, No 101239437. Project financed by the European Union. Jean Monnet Actions in the field of Higher Education: Modules. Call for tenders ERASMUS-JMO-2025-HEI-TCH-RSCH. As a student of the project, I have had the opportunity to study this directive thoroughly and I would therefore like to be able to give my opinion on it. I consider it very important to take into account the fact that we live in an era of constant technological advances that are developing, as do our society, which are relevant factors that need to be considered, as our reality is not the same as that that was years ago. This seems to me to have a particular impact in the case of minors, since for them social media and the virtual world in general are particularly affected, since they may be much more influenced and may not be able to differentiate what happens there from what happens in the real world, and it is therefore very important that there is more regulation for them in this regard. Elements such as the low age check we find on social media are one of the factors influencing the ease of access for minors to content that is not appropriate to their age, and addictive pactrons with which networks operate affect their mental health and cognitive development as they are dependent on them. All this is some of the issues that, in my view, it would be important to try to regulate more in order to try to provide better protection for children. Secondly, I also think it is important to mention the problem of buying products online, and as in many cases, although it is a purchase of a product, in the digital world it does not appear to work in the same way as in the physical world when it should not be the case. This is particularly true for video games, as it is not only the fact that you do not really have the full disposal of the product you bought, but they can even remove the game even though you have paid for it. Finally, I would like to clarify the case with regard to the terms and conditions of the websites. At the personal level, I never read the conditions I am marking because it is an issue that appears on all the pages I visit, as they are very long and tedious to read, and that is why I believe that there are not really many incentives we have to read them, so you might perhaps try to show some way in which the consumer is left with at least key points, although it is true that it is something residual and perhaps not as important or priority as other topics. In conclusion, in my view, this new law must be adapted to the new social reality in which we live, where a large majority of people have access to the digital world in a much easier and more common way, so laws need to adapt to this.
